scenarios.query:
Review this change. Project context: Ruby on Rails app; errors are raised as ServiceError(code) and rendered by a controller concern; RuboCop enforced.
```ruby
def cancel_subscription(id)
  sub = Subscription.find_by(id: id)
  if sub == nil
    return { error: 'not_found', code: 404 }
  end
  arr = Array.new
  sub.items.each { |i| arr.push(i.name) }
  arr
end
```
expected_behavior: Flags the ad-hoc error hash diverging from the project's ServiceError convention (a second error shape)
Flags the non-idiomatic nil check (sub == nil vs sub.nil? / unless sub) per the project's linter
Flags the clumsy Array.new + each + push where the idiomatic form is sub.items.map(&:name)
Flags the parallel utility duplicating the existing configured fetchJson helper (loses auth/retry behavior and creates a second way)
Flags the snake_case name in a camelCase codebase
Recommends using or extending the existing helper instead of adding a competitor
Recognizes the change follows the project's error convention and idiomatic pathlib usage
Does not impose personal style preferences on convention-following code
